stories.title: EU approves €90bn loan for Ukraine and fresh Russia sanctions – Europe live
stories.summary: Move comes after Hungary and Slovakia dropped opposition following reopening of the Druzhba oil pipeline Meanwhile, Ukraine’s president Volodymyr Zelenskiy said that Kyiv will seek to receive the first tranche of the €90bn European Union loan by the end of May, or early June. “This is strengthening of our army,” he told reporters in a WhatsApp chat, reported by Reuters. Continue reading...

stories.title: EU risks fallout with US over Trump-linked Balkans pipeline plan
stories.summary: Exclusive: Brussels seeks to stall awarding of contract to firm fronted by US president’s lawyer in letter seen by Guardian The EU risks a confrontation with Donald Trump after it sought to stall the awarding of a lucrative Balkans pipeline contract to a company fronted by his personal lawyer, documents seen by the Guardian show. Brussels has clashed with Trump over trade, Ukraine and military spending, but the intervention in the Southern Interconnection pipeline project appears to mark the fir

stories.title: EU approves €90B loan to Ukraine
stories.summary: BRUSSELS ― The EU agreed to release the €90 billion loan to Ukraine on Thursday following months of tense negotiations. “The European support loan for Ukraine has been unblocked ― €90 billion over two years,” Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy wrote on X . Hungary and Slovakia ― who had previously blocked the financial package ― failed to object before a 3 p.m. deadline, clearing the final hurdle to approve the loan. The two countries lifted their vetoes after oil flows through

stories.title: Newsletter: EU leaders weigh a NATO-style common defence clause
stories.summary: Also in this newsletter: EU unblocks €90bn Ukraine loan and new Russia sanctions.

stories.title: EU approves €90bn loan for Ukraine as pipeline is turned on ending deadlock
stories.summary: Ukraine says it has reopened the Druzhba pipeline, after months of stalemate over stalled oil supplies to neighbouring Hungary.

stories.title: Trump embere bedobta a FIFA-nak, hogy Irán helyett Olaszország vegyen részt a nyári foci-vb-n
stories.summary: Ezzel javítaná a Meloni és Trump között megromlott viszonyt.

stories.title: Párhuzamosan végezhetik rezidensi és doktori képzésüket a leendő orvosok
stories.summary: Ezt a lehetőséget nyitja meg a gyakorlatban is a Semmelweis Egyetem és a magyar egészségügy meghatározó intézményei között létrejött együttműködés.

stories.title: A Tiborcz-féle brókerbirodalmon belül is Orbán vereségére fogadtak nagy tétben a választás előtt
stories.summary: Az egyik equiloros alap nagy összegeket fektetett hosszú lejáratú magyar államkötvényekbe, április 12-e után rögtön csilingelt is a kassza.

stories.title: Nem lehet megbékélés, amíg a bűnöket nem ismerjük meg – élőben Szabó Andreával és Sz. Bíró Zoltánnal
stories.summary: Milyen lesz Magyar Péter és Putyin viszonya? Mik voltak a Szijjártó-interjú tanulságai? Milyen elszámoltatást akar a magyar társadalom? A 2026 élő adásában Sz. Bíró Zoltán Oroszország-szakértő és Szabó Andrea szociológus.

stories.title: Egy megtalálhatatlan vajdasági szervezet kapott 240 milliós támogatást a választások előtt
stories.summary: Az Agenda Polgárok Egyesületéről egyetlen cikk sem szerepel a vajdasági magyar online térben, nincs honlapja és nyilvánosan elérhető emailcíme sem.

stories.title: 66-25: Poll shows dramatic spike in Tisza's popularity since election
stories.summary: In contrast, the Fidesz camp has shrunk significantly. Such a dramatic turnaround within such a short period of time is unprecedented in Hungary, according to the head of Medián, Endre Hann.

stories.title: Arte: Egyes európai közmédiák a magyar köztévére intő példaként tekintenek
stories.summary: A közszolgálati média továbbra is az egyik legmegbízhatóbb információforrás Európában, de egyre nagyobb politikai és pénzügyi nyomás nehezedik rá.

stories.title: Magyar Péter kabinetfőnöke a Tisza egyik frakcióvezető-helyettese lesz
stories.summary: Velkey György László lesz az általános frakcióvezető-helyettese Bujdosó Andreának.

stories.title: A webáruház, aminek nem tudnak nemet mondani az autósok (x)
stories.summary: Ritka az olyan magabiztos menetelés a hazai e-kereskedelemben, mint a Kovács Autóalkatrészé. Az idei Ország Boltja versenyen nemcsak a jubileumi, tizedik szakmai díjukat hozták el, de a közönségszavazáson is az élre törtek. Megnéztük, mi áll a számok mögött, és hogyan lett egy szegedi kisboltból országos digitális platform.

stories.title: Ezek az idei slágertelepülések: hol, mennyiért vehetünk nyaralót? (x)
stories.summary: Ahogy megérkezik a jó idő, egyre többen kezdenek el játszani a gondolattal: milyen lenne egy saját nyaraló a vízparton. A legtöbbek fejében ilyenkor automatikusan a Balaton képe ugrik be, de Magyarország ennél jóval több lehetőséget kínál azoknak, akik vízparti kikapcsolódásra vágynak. A Zenga adatai alapján megnéztük, mely vízparti települések voltak a legkeresettebbek 2025-ben és 2026 eddigi részében, és azt is, hol, mennyibe kerül ma egy ilyen álomingatlan.

stories.title: Sz. Bíró Zoltán: Végtelen szervilizmust és kiszolgáltatottságot mutattak a Kreml felé Orbánék hangfelvételei
stories.summary: Az Oroszország-szakértő a Telex műsorában beszélt az átalakuló magyar–orosz kapcsolatokról, Orbánék korábbi hozzáállásáról, hamis zászlós műveletekről és Paks II.-ről is.

stories.title: Jogi atombombát jelentene az országnak, ha május 13-án véget érne a veszélyhelyzet
stories.summary: Több mint 150 rendelet vesztené hatályát, megszűnne a benzin- és élelmiszerárstop, de változna a javítóintézetek vagy a gödi akkumulátorgyár helyzete is. Ez azt is mutatja, miért nem jó, ha nem arra használják a veszélyhelyzetet, amire való. Különleges jogrendben élünk 2020 óta. Magyar azt kérte a távozó Orbán-kormánytól, hogy ezt május 31-ig hosszabbítsák meg.

stories.title: A magyar közélet még azt a közösséget is képes volt felbolygatni, aminek a célja az olvasás népszerűsítése
stories.summary: A TikTok köré szerveződő magyar közösség egyre nagyobb és népszerűbb, a magyar BookTok most megtartja az első fesztiválját, de nem mindenki örömére.

stories.title: His dance brought him global fame, but Zsolt Hegedűs wants to be known for reforming the ailing Hungarian healthcare system
stories.summary: The orthopedic specialist known for his Travolta-like moves plans to introduce the best elements of the British healthcare system as Minister of Health in the Tisza government.

stories.title: Szoboszlai Dominikot ötödször választották a hónap legjobb játékosának a Liverpool szurkolói
stories.summary: Február után márciusban is a magyar válogatott csapatkapitánya kapta a díjat.

stories.summary: A szociológus szerint a kormánypártnak nem lett volna szabad megpróbálnia kivonni az országot Európából, nyitnia kellett volna a fiatalok felé, és el kéne engednie a kirekesztő retorikát.

stories.title: Már a választás előtt csaknem ötéves csúcsra ugrott a magyarok gazdasági optimizmusa
stories.summary: A GKI üzleti és fogyasztói bizalmi indexe is nőni tudott április elején, a rossz hír csak az, hogy a felmérés szerint rengeteg cég készül éppen árat emelni.

stories.title: Egyhangúlag elfogadták az ukrán hitelhez hiányzó jogszabályt az EU kormányai
stories.summary: Az egyhangúlag jóváhagyott írásbeli eljárásban senki sem tiltakozott 24 órán belül, így elfogadták a jogszabályt, amelyet a magyar kormány az ukrán kőolajtranzit újraindulásáig blokkolt.

stories.title: Romániában életbe lép Európa legátfogóbb nőgyilkosság-törvénye
stories.summary: A jogszabály a tervek szerint a büntetés mellett a megelőzésre is hangsúlyt fektet majd.

stories.summary: A kormányzásról nem mondana le a párt, csak a miniszterelnököt távolítanák el.

stories.summary: A leendő miniszterelnök azt is közölte, hogy meg fogják nyitni az ügynökaktákat.

stories.summary: A magyar és a szlovák kormány az ukrán kőolajtranzit újraindulása miatt blokkolt, így csak két hónappal a tervezett határidő után tudták elfogadni az új büntetőintézkedéseket.

stories.title: Segít az erős forint, egyelőre olcsóbbak a nyári repülőjegyek, mint tavaly
stories.summary: A drága kerozin miatt egyre több légitársaság ritkítja járathálózatát, ugyanakkor a Magyarországról induló rövid távú nyári járatok többsége idén még mindig olcsóbb, mint tavaly.

stories.title: Ukraine says Russian oil to Hungary, Slovakia has restarted
stories.summary: Energy companies in Ukraine and Hungary have both said that oil flows through the Druzhba pipeline from Russia via Belarus have restarted. Hungary and Slovakia expect the first supplies to arrive by Thursday.

stories.title: EU unblocks €90 billion loan to Ukraine
stories.summary: Envoys from EU member states have finally paved the way for a €90 billion loan to Ukraine that had long been blocked by Hungary. They also approved a 20th package of sanctions on Russia over its invasion of its neighbor.

stories.title: Nine lessons for the US from Viktor Orbán’s defeat | Kenneth Roth
stories.summary: As US elections approach, the Hungarian prime minister’s loss is a reminder that history does not march relentlessly toward autocracy Viktor Orbán’s electoral loss was a slap in the face for Donald Trump and JD Vance, who had enthusiastically endorsed Europe’s most visible autocrat but proved unable to salvage his candidacy. But Hungarian voters’ 12 April rejection of Orbán also holds important lessons for Americans who hope to resist Trump’s own autocratic tendencies. As the November midterm el

stories.title: Hungary: Orban-era LGBTQ law infringes human rights, ECJ rules
stories.summary: The EU's top court has found that Hungary's 2021 laws on issues including transgender and homosexual identity and sex offenses breach various EU laws. This includes Article 2 of EU Treaty, to protect human dignity.

stories.title: Picasso’s Guernica is the ultimate emblem of the horrors of war. It has no place in Spain's partisan squabbles | María Ramírez
stories.summary: Forty years after the 1937 masterpiece returned to Madrid from its Franco-era exile in New York, it is again embroiled in politics Every September, Spain celebrates one of the most symbolic moments of its transition to democracy. This year will mark 45 years since an Iberia commercial flight from New York landed in Madrid with its pilot announcing to the surprised passengers that they had just travelled with one of the country’s most famous exiles: Pablo Picasso’s Guernica. After more than four

stories.title: Rat poison found in baby food, extortion suspected
stories.summary: Rat poison has been found in HiPP baby food jars across Austria, Czechia and Slovakia in what authorities suspect is a blackmail attempt.

stories.title: Ukraine hits Russian Black Sea port city, 1 dead
stories.summary: Russian authorities said one man was killed in a Ukrainian drone raid on city of Tuapse. Meanwhile, Russia arrested a German woman in an alleged bomb plot.

stories.title: Farage wants a picture of King Charles in every English school
stories.summary: LONDON — Reform UK said Thursday it would mandate every school in England to display a portrait of King Charles III and to fly the Union Jack if it wins power. In an announcement timed with England’s saint day, the right-wing populists — currently topping British polls — said they would make funding available for flagpoles, and require schools to observe St George’s Day. Nigel Farage’s party promised it would also introduce a “patriotic curriculum” requiring at least 60 percent

stories.title: Medical data of 500,000 people listed for sale on Chinese website
stories.summary: LONDON — Health data charity UK Biobank suffered a data breach that saw around 500,000 volunteers’ confidential information advertised for sale on Chinese e-commerce site Alibaba, DSIT minister Ian Murray told the Commons Thursday. Murray said the department became aware of the issue on Monday and that UK Biobank, which has close ties to the NHS, had suspended further access to the data at its request. The charity has referred itself to the Information Commissioner’s Office. “I

stories.title: Climat : sur la scène internationale, Monique Barbut assume de jouer petit bras
stories.summary: PARIS — Que se passe-t-il pour que la France n’envoie pas de ministre à la conférence de Santa Marta  sur la sortie des énergies fossiles qui se tient la semaine prochaine ? Et, alors que l’exécutif tricolore préside le G7 cette année, qu’il décide d’escamoter du menu des négociations des ministres de l’Environnement qui s’ouvre ce jeudi 23 avril l’énergie et la lutte contre le changement climatique ? Nommée au gouvernement en partie pour

stories.title: Italy’s migrant detention hubs in Albania not against EU law, says top EU court adviser
stories.summary: Italy’s migrant detention hubs in Albania are in line with EU law as long as migrants’ rights are fully protected, according to an adviser to the bloc’s top court. The legal opinion, which is not binding, is a win for the government of Giorgia Meloni, whose plans to process and deport asylum-seekers via offshore hubs have been embroiled in legal challenges. Italy’s center in Gjadër reportedly held 90 people in February, the highest number since Rome opened the hub, but fa

stories.title: Erdogan says Iran war ‘starting to weaken Europe’ – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed. Follow our new live blog on the Middle East crisis here If you’re just joining us, here’s the main news of the day. It is 9.30am in Tehran, 9am in Jerusalem and Beirut, and 2am in Washington DC. Donald Trump unilaterally said he is extending the ceasefire with Iran at Pakistan’s request while awaiting a “unified proposal” from Tehran , even as the US military maintains its blockade of Iranian ports. stories.title: Iran behind low-level ‘hybrid warfare’ attacks in UK and Europe, analysts say
stories.summary: Iranian intelligence apparently using intermediaries to sow fear with attacks frequently on Jewish targets Iranian intelligence services and Revolutionary Guards operatives are recruiting teenagers through criminal intermediaries to launch a wave of low-level “hybrid warfare” attacks in Europe and the UK, according to investigators, security officials, analysts and police documents. A first wave of attacks was launched in early March, 10 days after the US and Israel began strikes on Iran, and ta

stories.summary: President says forces can ‘shoot and kill’ any boat laying mines and the US has ‘total control’ of the strait ‘Impossible’ to reopen strait of Hormuz amid ‘flagrant’ ceasefire breaches, Iran says The Pentagon abruptly announced that the secretary of the US navy, John Phelan, would be leaving his job yesterday. stories.title: Germany news: Bavarian police arrest suspected spies
stories.summary: Two men have been arrested in Bavaria and charged with espionage, Frankfurt Airport's new Terminal 3 has opened its doors, and former US President Obama has praised a German climate activist. DW has more.

stories.title: Lebanon accuses Israel of targeting journalist killed in air strike
stories.summary: Lebanon's prime minister accused Israel of war crimes after IDF attacks on Red Cross vehicles also stopped rescuers from reaching the site.

stories.title: ‘A deal is a deal,’ Trump’s ambassador tells EU
stories.summary: DELPHI, Greece — The U.S. ambassador to the European Union called on Thursday for the EU-U.S. trade deal to be approved without major changes. “Our position is that a deal is a deal,” Andrew Puzder told POLITICO. The three EU institutions — the European Council, Parliament and Commission — are in talks to finalize enabling legislation for the deal struck at President Donald Trump’s Turnberry golf resort in Scotland last year. The agreement would oblige the bloc to eliminate tariffs on U.S. indus

stories.title: Exiled Iranian prince calls on Germany’s Merz to halt talks with regime in Tehran
stories.summary: BERLIN — Reza Pahlavi, the exiled son of Iran’s last shah, called on German Chancellor Friedrich Merz to abandon talks with Tehran as European leaders prepared to gather in Cyprus for a crisis summit with the war in Iran high on the agenda. Pahlavi — whose father’s repressive rule helped ignite the 1979 revolution and who has since emerged as a prominent, though deeply contested, opposition figure — made the remarks during a controversial visit to Berlin on Thursday, where he was scheduled to me

stories.title: Comment les Verts britanniques veulent purger leur programme pour mieux gagner
stories.summary: LONDRES – Pendant des années, le Green Party, le parti vert britannique, a évolué dans les broussailles du champ politique du Royaume-Uni, se concentrant sur les sujets environnementaux avec des résultats électoraux modestes. Mais il a connu une croissance inédite depuis l’arrivée à sa tête de “l’éco-populiste” Zack Polanski, qui souhaite désormais présenter sa formation comme une alternative progressiste et ambitieuse à la gauche du parti travailliste du Premier mi

stories.title: Former French PM Attal moves closer to presidential run with book launch
stories.summary: PARIS — The race to lead France’s center right into next year’s presidential election is increasingly turning into a battle between two of Emmanuel Macron’s former prime ministers, both of whom have since turned on the president. Gabriel Attal is touring France to promote his first book, “ En homme libre ” (“As a free man”). The 37-year-old briefly served as Macron’s prime minister in 2024 before being pushed aside by the French president’s s

stories.title: Pluralisme politique : qui veut la peau de l’Arcom ?
stories.summary: PARIS — “Y a-t-il encore un gendarme à la tête de l’Arcom ?” Cette question, soulevée début avril par la députée socialiste Ayda Hadizadeh en pleine commission d’enquête sur l’audiovisuel public, résume à elle seule la méfiance à l’égard du régulateur qui gagne les bancs de l’Assemblée. stories.title: Ben Roberts-Smith planned to leave Australia and researched buying wellness business in Spain, court documents show
stories.summary: Partner of soldier accused of war crimes says the couple discussed possibility of moving overseas to ‘create some normalcy in our lives’ Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Ben Roberts-Smith was planning to leave Australia to live overseas and had a business class flight out of the country booked in four days’ time when he was arrested at Sydney airport this month, court documents allege, with investigators telling a court “his willingness to return to Australia to face

stories.title: Governments failed to deliver $160m of river improvements including for now-parched NSW wetlands, report finds
stories.summary: NSW and Queensland governments ‘severely underdelivered’ on promised infrastructure to improve water flows, independent review finds Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Two state governments have drastically underdelivered more than $160m in infrastructure measures to improve river health in the northern Murray-Darling basin eight years since they were promised, a major independent review has found. This includes fai

stories.title: The international right has CPAC. Has the left finally found its answer? | Owen Jones
stories.summary: Spain’s PM, Pedro Sánchez, hosted the inaugural meeting of the Global Progressive Mobilisation. Keir Starmer and other social democrats were notable by their absence Can progressives push back the rising tide of authoritarianism? Thousands of people gathered in Barcelona this weekend in search of an answer. The occasion was the inaugural Global Progressive Mobilisation – an ambitious initiative backed by the Spanish prime minister, Pedro Sánchez – which drew an impressive cast: Brazil’s Lula, Me

stories.title: Bibby Stockholm asylum barge contractor admits overcharging UK government £118m
stories.summary: Australia’s Corporate Travel Management is ‘negotiating commercial arrangements’ to refund the money The Australian company that ran the Bibby Stockholm asylum barge has admitted it overcharged the British government by £118m. Corporate Travel Management (CTM) said its auditor had found evidence of “erroneous billing” of its UK clients, increasing its estimate of how much it owes the government by £40m. Continue reading...

stories.title: Spanish conservatives revive regional pacts with far-right Vox party
stories.summary: Hard line on immigration adopted by People’s party as right seeks to overthrow socialist government in 2027 Europe live – latest updates Spain’s opposition conservatives are rekindling their regional pacts with the far-right Vox party by adopting the latter’s hard line on immigration. It comes less than two years after disagreements over the issue led to the collapse of coalition administrations in five of the country’s self-governing regions. The renewal of the regional deals between the People

stories.title: Philippines’ ex-president Rodrigo Duterte to face trial for crimes against humanity
stories.summary: ICC judges say there are substantial grounds to believe Duterte guided anti-drugs crackdown that killed thousands The former president of the Philippines, Rodrigo Duterte , will face trial at the international criminal court (ICC) after judges unanimously confirmed charges of crimes against humanity over his “war on drugs”. Pre-trial judges concluded on Thursday that there were substantial grounds to believe Duterte was responsible for the crimes against humanity of murder and attempted murder i

stories.title: Starmer brushes off reports that cabinet ministers don’t support him over sacking of Olly Robbins – UK politics live
stories.summary: The prime minister said that cabinet is still united and focused on delivering the government’s policy agenda Little says in March she had a meeting when she asked to see the Foreign Office’s documentation about the decision to grant Mandelson vetting. She said she was asking because this was documentation covered by the humble address. She said was told that “that information would not be forthcoming”. In the middle of March, I have a meeting with Sir Olly and a senior member of his team, and t

stories.title: Senate Republicans bypass Democrats to advance $70bn ICE and border patrol plan in ‘vote-a-rama’– US politics live
stories.summary: Republicans deploy measure known as budget reconciliation to push through plan without backing from Democrats during lengthy late-night vote Sign up for the Breaking News US email You’ve likely seen that the Senate adopted the plan for the budget blueprint for ICE and border patrol after an all-night “vote-a-rama”. This is, in fact, not a congressional dance break. Continue reading...

stories.title: Reza Pahlavi calls for regime change in Iran on Berlin visit
stories.summary: The former Shah's son is in Germany to drum up support for his global campaign to overthrow Iran's leadership. During a speech in the capital, he called the German government's refusal to meet with him "a disgrace."

stories.title: Iran war: Trump orders 'shoot and kill' on mine-laying boats in Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Tensions over the Strait of Hormuz continue as the US president increases threats. Iranian leaders have said there can be no peace talks while the US blockade of Iranian ports continues. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Opinion: The courage to speak
stories.summary: At the Antalya Diplomacy Forum, my experience as a rabbi showed that even small moments of dialogue can begin to rebuild fragile bridges between communities.

stories.title: South African court orders Zambia to return former president's body
stories.summary: Zambia authorities and Edgar Lungu's family are in a dispute over where the former leader's remains should be buried.

stories.title: France holds national tribute for soldier killed in Lebanon
stories.summary: The Minister for the Armed Forces, Catherine Vautrin, the Chief of Staff of the Army and General Pierre Schill preside over a national tribute ceremony in Montauban to honour the memory of Staff Sergeant Florian Montorio.

stories.title: Kazakhstan confirms suspension of its oil transit to Germany via Russia
stories.summary: Kazakhstan’s energy minister announced Wednesday there would be no transit of its oil to Germany through Russia throughout May due to Russian technical constraints, assuming it is “related to recent strikes on Russian infrastructure".

stories.title: UN rights chief alarmed by number of drugs-related executions in Singapore
stories.summary: Officials in Singapore maintain that the death penalty has made it one of the world's safest cities and a government survey in 2023 showed strong public support for capital punishment for serious crimes.

stories.title: Two US officials who died after Mexico drug raid reported to be CIA agents
stories.summary: Mexico to investigate possible breach of its constitution and assess US’s role in anti-drug operation near Chihuahua Mexico has launched an investigation into a possible breach of its constitution as it was reported that two US embassy officials who died in a car accident while returning from a raid on a drug lab with local officials in the border state of Chihuahua were CIA operatives. The accident happened early on Sunday, as the officials were driving back from the scene of the raid. Their ve

stories.title: Number of billionaires globally could reach 4,000 in next five years
stories.summary: There are now 3,110 billionaires but analysis shows ‘deep structural acceleration’ in wealth creation around world The number of billionaires in the world could reach nearly 4,000 by 2031, figures suggest, as the super-rich accumulate wealth at an accelerating rate. There are now 3,110 billionaires globally, according to analysis by the estate agent Knight Frank. This is forecast to rise by 25% over the next five years, taking the total to 3,915. Continue reading...

stories.title: Pilot’s selfie led to mid-air collision in F-15K fighter jet, says South Korea’s air force
stories.summary: One pilot ordered to repay some of the $600,000 of damage caused by collision in 2021 South Korea’s air force has apologised for a 2021 mid-air collision involving two fighter jets, a day after auditors said pilots were taking selfies and filming during the flight and held them responsible for the accident. “We sincerely apologise to the public for the concern caused by the accident that occurred in 2021,” an air force spokesperson said in a press briefing. The spokesperson said one of the pilot

stories.title: Has the world grown weary of art biennials? In search of an antidote, a Portuguese festival turns to anarchism
stories.summary: Art festivals can fill abandoned buildings with new life – or clear a path for property developers. Coimbra’s Anozero is trying out a more confrontational approach If you decide to spend a night at Coimbra’s Monastery of Santa Clara-a-Nova in the near future, do bear in mind that the place is almost certainly haunted. Disembodied children’s voices echo around the first floor of the 17th century convent perched atop a hill in the Portuguese university city, overlooking the medieval centre from ac

stories.title: David Malouf, Australian author of Remembering Babylon and Ransom, dies aged 92
stories.summary: Acclaimed Brisbane-born writer was known for his work exploring his own childhood, great myths and colonial Australia Obituary: David Malouf, Australian writer whose work spanned the ancient world and 70s Brisbane David Malouf, the acclaimed Australian author of books including Ransom, An Imaginary Life and the Booker prize-nominated Remembering Babylon, has died aged 92. Malouf died on Wednesday, his publisher, Penguin Random House Australia, said in a statement on Thursday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Rinehart’s $200m donation to convert homes for veterans welcomed by RSL – as it happened

stories.summary: World’s worst nuclear disaster leaves mixed legacy of nature’s resilience amid serious contamination, as wars increase lobbying for energy supply Forty years on from the world’s worst nuclear disaster, Chornobyl is still contaminated with almost half the caesium-137 that exploded from the Unit 4 reactor in 1986, as well as much longer-lived hazards such as plutonium, tritium and americium. But according to some experts, the long-term affects on nature may be less than if the area had been left t

stories.title: Head-on train collision near Copenhagen leaves five critically injured
stories.summary: At least 18 people hurt after crash involving two local services north of Denmark’s capital Two trains have collided head-on in Denmark, injuring at least 18 people, five of whom are in a critical condition. The crash happened on Thursday morning at a level crossing at Isterødvejen, near Hillerød, a town about 19 miles (30km) north-west of Copenhagen. Emergency services received a report of the collision just before 6.30am. Continue reading...

stories.title: California officials tout historic drop in violence as killings fall to record low
stories.summary: State’s strict gun policies heralded as data shows 35% reduction in homicides between 2022 and 2024 California officials are touting a historic three-year decrease in homicides and gun violence that has led to the state’s lowest number of killings on record. The number of homicides in California decreased by 35% between 2022 and 2024, with 2,304 deaths reported in 2022 and 1,768 in 2024, according data from the US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention. stories.summary: Bills are response to election petitions challenging Godwin Friday and fellow politician’s eligibility to be MPs because of dual citizenship The St Vincent and the Grenadines government has delayed a controversial effort to amend a section of the country’s constitution that the opposition says renders the prime minister ineligible for his position in parliament. The government borrowed a net total of £132bn for the financial year ending in March, the Office for National Statistics (ONS) said. This slightly under

stories.title: Police and ombudsman investigate death of boy, 16, at young offender institution
stories.summary: Teenager found unresponsive at Feltham YOI, the first such death in England or Wales since 2019 Police and the prisons ombudsman are investigating the death of a 16-year-old boy who was being held at a young offender institution in south-west London. The boy was found unresponsive at Feltham YOI on Monday night. He was treated by paramedics before being rushed to hospital but died shortly before midnight. Continue reading...

stories.title: Lebanese journalist killed in Israeli attack had spoken of death threat

Amal Khalil , 43, who worked for al-Akhbar newspaper and had described herself as supportin

stories.title: Eighteen wolves found dead in Italian national park in suspected poisoning
stories.summary: Criminal investigation under way after carcasses found across Abruzzo, Lazio and Molise national park within a week The carcasses of 18 wolves have been found in an Italian national park within the space of a week in an apparent series of poisonings described by conservationists as the most serious crimes against wildlife in Italy in a decade. Authorities of the national park of Abruzzo, Lazio and Molise said eight wolves were found dead in recent days in three different areas of the vast park,

stories.title: Private health records of half a million Britons offered for sale on Chinese website
stories.summary: Technology minister tells Commons ‘de-identified’ information from UK Biobank advertised for sale on Alibaba UK politics live – latest updates The confidential health records of half a million British volunteers have been offered for sale on Chinese website Alibaba, the UK government has confirmed. The data, belonging to participants in the UK Biobank project, was found for sale on three separate listings last week. The records have now been removed and it is not believed any sales were made. Co

stories.title: ‘Impossible’ to reopen strait of Hormuz amid ‘flagrant’ ceasefire breaches, Iran says
stories.summary: Iranian forces seize two ships in critical waterway as Washington and Tehran maintain separate blockades Middle East crisis – live updates Iranian forces have seized two ships in the strait of Hormuz as the US and Iran doubled down on imposing separate blockades of the shipping waterway. The standoff over the strait – through which about 20% of the world’s oil and liquefied fossil gas passed through during peacetime – has raised doubts about whether stalled peace negotiations will resume. stories.title: Palantir: Why its political manifesto is causing a stir
stories.summary: The US tech company has posted a 22-point manifesto on X that goes well beyond typical corporate communications. Critics say it is evidence of "technofascism."

stories.title: Denmark: Two trains collide injuring several
stories.summary: Two trains have crashed into each other in Denmark, leaving several people critically injured, authorities have said.

stories.title: Lebanon says Israel targeted journalist killed in airstrike
stories.summary: Amal Khalil was killed when a house she was sheltering in was hit by an Israeli airstrike. Lebanon has accused Israel of targeting media workers as part of an "established approach" — a charge Israel denies.

stories.title: Prince Harry makes surprise visit to Ukraine
stories.summary: Prince Harry has arrived in Kyiv, ahead of taking part in the Kyiv Security Forum. His trip comes as Ukrainians fear they could be forgotten with the world's attention turned to the US conflict with Iran.

stories.title: Bayern Munich on the brink of historic 'double treble'
stories.summary: With Bundesliga titles in the bag and cup finals to come, Bayern's men and women have a chance to achieve something unique in Europe. The two teams dominate in Germany and appear united in their Champions League aims.

stories.summary: As UNESCO celebrates World Book Day on April 23, we visit a Scottish coastal town that was saved by books.

stories.title: Amnesty International paints a grim picture for human rights
stories.summary: In its 2025/2026 report, the human rights watchdog Amnesty International calls out the "predatory behavior of the powerful" and calls for action to defend the global order.

stories.title: Unpacking the 2026 Venice Biennale controversies and highlights
stories.summary: Following the return of Russia to the Biennale, the EU said it will carry out its threat to cut its funding from the international art show.

stories.title: How Ukraine is rebuilding its energy system under fire
stories.summary: Since Chernobyl, Ukraine has lived in the shadow of nuclear disaster. Now, with Russia targeting its power grid, it's facing a new nuclear risk. The response is reshaping how the world thinks about energy in wartime.

stories.summary: The two days of talks started with the approval of a massive loan for Ukraine and are also expected to address the EU budget and the fallout from the conflict in the Middle East.

stories.summary: During a visit to four African countries, Pope Leo XIV called for peace, unity and dialogue. He criticized war, corruption and inequality, urging leaders to put people first.

stories.title: Libya oscillates between cooperation and illusion

Janet Fordham was cheated of her life savings and her home over a period of five years by fraudsters apparently based in the UK, Germany, the US and Ghana, the inques

stories.title: Israeli strike kills journalist after ongoing attacks blocked rescuers, Lebanon says
stories.summary: Amal Khalil had been buried in rubble after an Israeli strike that also injured another journalist, Zeinab Faraj Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon killed a journalist on Wednesday after rescuers were blocked from accessing the building where she was buried under rubble because of further Israeli fire, according to several witnesses. stories.title: Germany's finance minister wants to scrap spousal tax splitting

stories.title: School's out in Iran: No phone, no internet, no classes
stories.summary: Iran's schools are closed because of the war. Classes are being held via TV programs and the national intranet. Children without a laptop or smartphone are left out. The country is effectively cut off from the internet.

A városi minierdők kiválóan mérséklik a klímaváltozás hatásait: csökkentik a hőmérsékletet, növelik a biodiverzitást, javítják a levegőminőséget és a közérzetünket is.

stories.title: Taiwan president blames China for forced cancellation of Eswatini trip

Lai was to leave on Wednesday for the 40th anniversary of King Mswati III’s accession. Continue reading...

stories.title: Millions in India stripped of vote before critical state election, as government seeks to ‘purify’ electoral roll
stories.summary: Experts say Muslims and other minorities have been disproportionately deleted from the electoral roll ahead of the West Bengal elections this week Millions of people in the Indian state of West Bengal have been stripped of their vote ahead of a critical state election this week, after a controversial electoral revision described by critics as a “bloodless political genocide” and mass disenfranchisement of minorities. stories.title: Heatwaves, floods and wildfires pose rising threat to democracy, report finds
stories.summary: Research shows natural hazards linked to climate crisis disrupted 23 elections in 18 countries in 2024 Democracy is under mounting threat from the climate crisis, with new analysis documenting how elections are increasingly shaped not only by political forces but also by floods, wildfires and extreme weather. At least 94 elections and referendums across 52 countries have been disrupted by climate-related impacts over the last two decades, researchers found. Continue reading...

stories.title: El Salvador holds mass trial for 486 alleged members of notorious MS-13 gang
stories.summary: Human rights groups have warned that the collective prosecutions violate due process and block defendants from accessing legal counsel A Salvadoran court on Tuesday began a collective trial of 486 alleged gang members, in one of the biggest mass trials under president Nayib Bukele’s crackdown on gang violence through controversial emergency powers. stories.title: Israeli ambassador: 'Only a strong Israel can achieve peace'

stories.summary: Qatar has urged diplomacy on Tuesday as uncertainty surrounds US-Iran talks, warning prolonged tensions risk wider global fallout, particularly if disruption in the Strait of Hormuz affects energy flows and markets.

stories.title: Israeli soldiers punished over vandalism of Jesus statue in Lebanon

stories.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu had said on Monday he was "stunned and saddened to learn that an IDF soldier damaged a Catholic religious icon in southern Lebanon."

stories.title: German police launch mass raids of 50 premises to smash Syrian trafficking ring
stories.summary: Police believe the ring operated by using genuine residency documents issued to Syrian refugees in Germany, which were sent to people in Syria with a similar appearance.

stories.title: UAE-backed Colombian mercenaries provided support to Sudan paramilitary, report says
stories.summary: The Conflict Insights Group (CIG) says its research also shows the extent of UAE involvement.

stories.title: Synthetic drugs and digital crime: Why Central Asia is emerging as a new frontline
stories.summary: The rise of synthetic drugs and digital trafficking methods is forcing a shift in global anti-narcotics strategies. Uzbekistan is advancing new initiatives to improve cross-border cooperation, financial oversight and public awareness.

stories.title: South Korean fighter jets collided due to pilots taking pictures, report finds
stories.summary: One of the pilots has been fined thousands of dollars by the military to cover repair costs.

stories.title: Iran executes former atomic agency employee amid Mossad spy claims
stories.summary: Iran executes former atomic energy employee Mehdi Farid for alleged Mossad links as rights groups decry opaque trial, coerced confessions and media blackout.

stories.title: Russia faked economic data to appear more resilient to its war and sanctions, intel report says
stories.summary: Swedish intelligence said Russia has been covering up a weak, debt-driven economy hit by war costs and sanctions, warning of rising inflation and banking risks.

stories.title: UK agriculture deal with EU will not remove all red tape, peers told
stories.summary: Lords told sales of Scottish shellfish among areas that may benefit – but agreement will not erase all paperwork Europe live – latest updates A new agriculture agreement with the EU will not wipe out all Brexit paperwork but might pave the way for sales of Scottish langoustines and oysters, the House of Lords has heard. The UK and EU are close to finalising a sanitary and phytosanitary (SPS) agreement to reduce Brexit trade barriers, and while it will have “modest” impact on the UK economy the a

stories.title: Carney names broad team to advise on tense US-Canada trade talks
stories.summary: Conservatives and former provincial premiers among those PM names to advisory committee on economic relations Canada’s prime minister, Mark Carney , says his new advisory committee on economic relations with the United States will draw on the “best advice and the broadest perspectives” as the country braces for what many expect will be tense trade negotiations with its southern neighbour. The 24-member advisory committee, announced on Tuesday, shows the prime minister’s eagerness to reach across

stories.title: Trump officials consider sending 1,100 Afghans who aided US forces to Congo
stories.summary: Discussions reportedly come after Trump’s decision to stop initiative that allowed group to apply to resettle in the US The Trump administration is in discussions to potentially send up to 1,100 Afghans who helped US forces during the war in Afghanistan to the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), a non-profit confirmed on Tuesday. The resettlement talks, first reported by the New York Times , come after Donald Trump’s decision to stop an initiative that allowed Afghans who assisted US war efforts

stories.title: Condom prices could rise 30% due to Iran war, says world’s top producer Karex
stories.summary: Karex produces more than 5 billion condoms annually and is a supplier to leading brands like Durex and Trojan, as well as the NHS The world’s top condom producer, Malaysia’s Karex Bhd, plans to raise prices by 20% to 30% and possibly further if supply chain disruptions drag on due to the Iran war, its chief executive has said. Karex is also seeing a surge in condom demand as rising freight costs and shipping delays have left many of its customers with lower stockpiles than usual, CEO Goh Miah Ki

stories.title: Mars: NASA's Curiosity rover finds new organic matter in Gale Crater
stories.summary: NASA's Curiosity rover has identified more organic compounds as scientists investigate any potential ancient life signs on Mars. It was exploring the massive Gale Crater also believed to be a now-dry lake.

stories.title: UK moves to ban smoking for everyone born after 2008
stories.summary: A draft law in the UK to create a "smoke-free generation" by banning smoking for anybody born after 2008 has cleared both houses of parliament. Only the king's signature remains for it to become law.

stories.title: How grievance turns to violence in a mass shooter's mind
stories.summary: Attacks like last week's school shooting in Turkey can seem to come out of nowhere, but they rarely do. Instead, a buildup of grievance and fixation often precedes the the moment violent thoughts become violent acts.

stories.title: Record number of immigrants reside in EU in 2025
stories.summary: The foreign‑born population of the EU grew to around 64 million in 2025. Germany remains the biggest host of immigrants in absolute terms, but some smaller nations have a much higher percentage of foreign-born residents.

stories.title: Iran war: Jet fuel crisis deepens as Lufthansa cuts flights
stories.summary: The impact of jet fuel shortages because of the closure of the Strait of Hormuz is starting to burn. Lufthansa has canceled 20,000 flights and experts warn more cancellations and price hikes are on the way.

stories.title: Could Iran war trigger bigger trade crisis than COVID?
stories.summary: As the Iran war simmers, trade experts ask whether it could reshape supply chains more deeply than COVID-19, forcing companies to rethink strategies to counter further geopolitical shocks.

stories.title: Avoid men, German police association chief tells women
stories.summary: Police association chief Dirk Peglow has stirred debate with his advice to women to avoid relationships with men for safety's sake. But his provocative tip is not without reason, as statistics show.

stories.title: The rise and fall of East Germany's Palace of the Republic
stories.summary: From 1976 through 1990, the building in East Berlin served as a symbol of the communist state's power. The "People's Palace" was controversially destroyed — and yet remains present.

stories.title: Russia to block Kazakh oil flows to Germany via key pipeline
stories.summary: Germany has confirmed that Russia will block flows of Kazakh oil to a vital refinery that supplies much of Berlin's energy needs from May 1.

stories.title: Humpback whale Timmy remains stranded in shallow waters in northern Germany
stories.summary: A stranded humpback whale in the Baltic Sea remains stuck near Poel island, Germany, as rescuers struggle to guide it back to deeper waters.

stories.title: Iran sees mass redundancies from war with US and Israel
stories.summary: The impact is being felt by manufacturers, retailers and the digital sector, amid warnings it could get worse if the war resumes.

stories.title: Mexico's president urges tighter security at tourist sites ahead of World Cup after shooting
stories.summary: Security officials said that the shooting at the world-famous Teotihuacan pyramids that left one person dead and 13 others wounded "wasn't spontaneous."

stories.title: Six stand trial in Istanbul over insecticide poisoning deaths of Turkish-German family
stories.summary: The Turkish-German Böcek family were staying at the Harbour Suites Old City hotel in Istanbul’s Fatih district when they fell ill on 12 November, the state-run Anadolu news agency said.

stories.title: From Epstein to sock puppets: Key takeaways from Kevin Warsh's Fed confirmation hearing
stories.summary: He denied making a deal with Donald Trump on interest rates and backed "policy regime change" at the central bank.

stories.title: Almost 8,000 people died or disappeared on migration routes in 2025, IOM says
stories.summary: The migration agency highlighted that at least around 340,000 family members are estimated to have been directly affected by the missing migrant crisis since 2014.

stories.title: UK ambassador to UAE warns of regional risk but backs UAE resilience amid fragile Iran ceasefire
stories.summary: In an interview with Euronews, British Ambassador to the United Arab Emirates Edward Hobart said the fragile US-Iran ceasefire should pave the way to a lasting peace with "a clear way forward".

stories.title: A Kashmir tourist hotspot became a bloodbath. A year on, the pain remains unbearable
stories.summary: A year after militants killed 26 people in Indian-administered Kashmir, families are trying to cope with their loss.

stories.title: Why police are seeking to arrest billionaire K-pop mogul behind BTS
stories.summary: Bang Si-hyuk, who created the supergroup, denies defrauding investors before his $7.3bn company went public.

stories.title: Trump buys time for Iran deal after frantic day of diplomacy
stories.summary: The US president's decision marked the second time in as many weeks he has backed off a threat to escalate the war, buying more time.

stories.title: US singer D4vd pleads not guilty to murder in death of missing teen girl
stories.summary: Prosecutors say the singer, real name David Anthony Burke, could face life in prison due to the nature of the case, if convicted.

stories.title: Iran seizes two cargo ships in Strait of Hormuz after three vessels fired on
stories.summary: The tanker seizures come after US President Donald Trump said the ceasefire with Iran was being indefinitely extended at the request of mediator Pakistan.

stories.title: Thirteen killed in second India fireworks blast in three days
stories.summary: Earlier this week, an explosion at a firecracker factory in neighbouring state Tamil Nadu left at least 25 dead.

stories.title: Taiwan president cancels trip after African countries revoke flight permits
stories.summary: Taiwan has accused Beijing of putting pressure on African countries to close their airspace to Lai's plane.

stories.title: Central Asian leaders gather at first ever regional climate summit to discuss water crisis
stories.summary: The Regional Ecological Summit kicks off today in Astana.

stories.title: Israelis mark Memorial Day during ongoing conflict
stories.summary: Israelis marked Memorial Day with ceremonies and informal tributes, reflecting on loss while continuing daily life amid ongoing conflict and uncertainty.

stories.title: US charges anti-extremism organisation over payments to informants in hate groups
stories.summary: The Southern Poverty Law Center said it would "vigorously defend ourselves, our staff, and our work".

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV condemns ‘lust for power’ and inequality in Equatorial Guinea
stories.summary: The Holy Father of the Catholic Church made a stop in the final leg of his four-country tour of Africa. Equatorial Guinea is a secular country, with around three-fourths of its population Catholic.
